authoritative_sources.title: Workforce Planning Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority. Its workforce-planning toolkit defines demand, supply, gap, and action-planning stages that constrain recommendation-safe appointment scheduling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: demand framing, capacity-gap analysis, and action-plan packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Remote Service Businesses
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR is a primary cosmetology regulator. Its remote-service business guidance is directly relevant to beauty appointment scheduling because it addresses app-based booking, pre-appointment information, and evidence expectations for remote or mobile services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: appointment intake grounding, mobile-service prerequisites, and disclosure-aware booking

authoritative_sources.title: Barbering and Cosmetology Inspections Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR's inspections guide is a direct operating reference for salon and spa readiness, including facility requirements, blood-exposure kits, notices, and sanitation conditions that determine whether appointment capacity is real.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: facility readiness, sanitation gating, and room or station availability validation

authoritative_sources.title: Establishment Forms and Publications
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR's forms page publishes regulator-approved health and safety materials and the Foot Spa Cleaning and Disinfecting Record, so it is authoritative for the evidence artifacts that gate beauty-service readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence sufficiency review for pedicure and sanitation-dependent bookings

authoritative_sources.title: Scope of Practice Guides
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR scope guides are a direct public authority boundary reference for matching beauty services to the correct license type rather than letting a scheduler improvise scope or assignment.
used_for: adjacent-lane routing for licensed scope issues

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Spas
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR's medical-spa guidance is a direct public boundary source for when a beauty booking crosses into physician-supervised or clinically governed work that this lane cannot approve.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: adjacent authority boundary review and med-spa handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Nail Salons
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's nail-salon guidance is a direct public worker-safety reference for salon operations and helps distinguish appointment-feasible work from conditions that require different staffing, controls, or escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: worker-safety-aware scheduling and same-day staffing posture

authoritative_sources.title: Nail Salons - Biological Hazards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's biological-hazards page is a direct public control reference for blood exposure, reusable implements, and contaminated materials, which changes whether stations and tools can be returned to the schedule safely.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: incident-driven schedule blocking and sanitation re-entry checks

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.1200 - Hazard Communication
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's Hazard Communication standard is the governing public baseline for labels and safety data sheets tied to treatment and disinfectant products used in beauty settings.

authoritative_sources.title: Recommended Cleaning and Disinfection Procedures for Foot Spa Basins in Salons
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA regulates antimicrobial pesticide products. Its salon foot-spa guidance is the direct public workflow reference for basin cleaning and disinfection, which materially affects pedicure-slot readiness.

workflow_stages.name: Demand and appointment-intake normalization
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the request is actually beauty appointment scheduling and normalize service-menu, duration, provider, room, waitlist, deposit, and planning-horizon context into one scheduling frame.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and provider-eligibility validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm intake, waiver, patch-test, contraindication-routing, minors-consent, provider-license fit, and remote-service or mobile-service prerequisites before a slot is treated as schedulable.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess provider availability, room or station capacity, turnaround buffers, sanitation dependencies, equipment outages, service-level commitments, and approved overbook or waitlist rules.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, sequence, and booking-readiness recommendation support
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only sequencing, gap-fill, rebooking, deferral, and waitlist outputs using approved tie-break rules and named beauty records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Sanitation, maintenance, and same-day exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Withdraw or revise tentative commitments when sanitation logs, blood-exposure events, chemical-readiness issues, or room or equipment outages emerge and route to the correct owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Licensed, clinical, financial, or policy-boundary routing
workflow_stages.description: Keep treatment eligibility, med-spa boundaries, refunds, fee waivers, policy overrides, and safety-sensitive decisions explicit and route them to the authorized owner when they determine the answer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: service menus, duration matrices, add-on pairings, turnaround buffers, and provider block rules
appointment books, waitlists, no-show histories, deposit records, and late-arrival policy extracts
provider rosters, license or certification records, scope maps, shift availability, and role assignments
digital intake, waiver, patch-test, allergy, contraindication-routing, minors-consent, and prep-instruction records
treatment-room calendars, station assignments, pedicure-chair readiness logs, sanitation checklists, and foot-spa cleaning records
maintenance tickets, out-of-service notices, incident reports, and escalation or approval-needed summaries
systems_of_record: POS or package and deposit ledger
room, station, and resource calendar system

allowed: Classify scheduling work as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Assess whether available records support a bounded schedule, routing, rebooking, or allocation recommendation.
Produce evidence-backed schedule recommendations, readiness summaries, constraint logs, and escalation packages without claiming final approval authority.
escalate: Required booking, provider, intake, patch-test, sanitation, maintenance, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
A licensed-practice, medical-spa, refund, fee-waiver, safety, or payroll condition changes whether work may proceed.
The request requires final operational, financial, legal, clinical, or policy-override authority.
Public baseline guidance and tenant policy materially conflict on provider eligibility, mobile-service handling, sanitation posture, or schedule precedence.
refuse: Commit final staffing, pricing, payroll, refund, treatment-eligibility, or operational approval.
Treat generic calendars or shift rosters as sufficient substitutes for service menus, provider-scope records, intake readiness, and sanitation evidence.
Book through missing patch-test, consent, sanitation, or maintenance blockers to preserve utilization or guest convenience.

domain_failure_modes: booking a service to a provider whose documented license or scope does not match the requested service
committing a pedicure, lash, waxing, or chemical-service slot without the required intake, patch-test, or sanitation evidence
treating room or station availability in the booking calendar as authoritative even when maintenance or sanitation systems say the resource is out of service
letting same-day revenue pressure override turnaround buffers, implement-cleaning controls, or blood-exposure handling
collapsing the lane into generic calendar management without service-menu, add-on, room-turn, or waitlist semantics
allowing a beauty booking to drift into medical-spa or licensed clinical eligibility decisions because the guest is already in the schedule
using tenant convenience scripts to bypass mobile-service disclosure or provider-boundary rules that materially change the safe answer

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Patch-test timing, minors-consent rules, package-booking rules, and deposit or no-show posture vary materially by jurisdiction, service line, and tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline establishes that these facts can change booking safety and validity, so runtime must retrieve tenant and jurisdiction policy before deterministic recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Remote or mobile beauty-service obligations do not apply uniformly to every tenant or location, and med-spa adjacency varies by service mix.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This is handled by making service type, jurisdiction, mobile-service posture, and med-spa context mandatory runtime facts before applying those controls.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, systems, and refusal behavior instead of generic scheduling mechanics only.

The lane remains recommendation-only even when it handles same-day callouts, waitlists, or utilization pressure; final staffing, clinical, financial, and policy-override authorities stay outside the lane.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated use because all known variation is explicitly managed through runtime retrieval and escalation requirements.
